mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Oracle's cloud capabilities without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This step installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the OCI CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud. Adjust the parameters as needed for your environment.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual IDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region> --token-version 2.0.0

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual cluster ID and region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster, as well as RBAC for security.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/oci/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/main/helm-charts/kube-prometheus-stack/templates/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command deploys the cluster autoscaler.
> This command sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs that may remain after cluster deletion.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id <your_load_balancer_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_load_balancer_id> with your actual load balancer ID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.1

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the nodes are running the new version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check that the nodes reflect the new version.

upgrade.title: Rollback if Necessary
upgrade.description:
If the upgrade fails or issues arise, you may need to rollback to the previous version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.0

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running OCI CLI commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the OCI CLI is not properly authenticated.

**Fix:**
Ensure your OCI CLI is configured with the correct credentials and permissions.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'NotReady'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the nodes are still initializing or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node status in the OCI console and ensure network configurations are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured VCN settings.

**Fix:**
Verify that the IAM policies allow load balancer creation and check VCN configurations.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl apply fails with 'context deadline exceeded'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a connectivity issue with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new OCI features such as improved load balancer configurations and enhanced security policies.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older API versions for load balancer management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure to review the new security policies and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
